engineIntelligence.h2: Renault 12 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main Cléon-Fonte engines fitted to the Renault 12 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

knownFailures: Head gasket degradation - common on Cléon-Fonte blocks exceeding 40 years, causes coolant/oil mixing.
Timing chain tensioner wear - leads to cold-start rattle and potential chain skip on high-mileage units.
Rear main seal oil leaks - age-related seal hardening causes external oil seepage from engine rear.

cards.altText: Renault 12 C1J engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carburettor ethanol damage - modern E10 fuel degrades vintage Weber/Solex components, causing poor running.
Cooling system corrosion - aluminium cylinder head susceptible to scale buildup if coolant not maintained.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,100-£1,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Cléon-Fonte units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Renault 12 C2L engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: High-compression piston wear - Gordini-spec units require precise clearances; wear causes power loss and knocking.
Twin Weber carburettor sync issues - complex induction system demands specialist tuning for optimal performance.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,600-£2,400 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Cléon-Fonte performance units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Renault 12 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every Cléon-Fonte engine code fitted to the Renault 12 across all UK generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production revision of 810 with updated cylinder head casting; functionally identical fitment for TL/TS variants.
compatibleVariants: TL (late production), TS (higher compression tune), TR (automatic transmission)
commonFailures: Cylinder head warping - overheating can distort aluminium head, requires pressure testing during rebuild.
Carburettor ethanol damage - modern E10 fuel degrades vintage Weber 32DIR components.
entries.cta: Get quotes for C1H Renault 12 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Bored-out 1.4L variant primarily for Argentine/Turkish export markets; requires manifold verification for UK fitment.
compatibleVariants: Argentine export models, Turkish Toros, Dacia 1300 compatible applications
commonFailures: Intake manifold bolt pattern differences - export models may not align with UK-spec components without adaptation.
Cooling system corrosion - aluminium head susceptible to scale if silicate-free antifreeze not used.
entries.cta: Get quotes for C1J Renault 12 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: High-output 1.6L Cléon-Fonte for TS performance variants; strengthened internals support higher compression tuning.
compatibleVariants: TS (performance trim), select high-spec Estate applications
commonFailures: High-compression piston wear - requires precise ring/end-gap clearances; wear causes power loss and knocking.
Upgraded cooling system requirement - performance tuning demands enhanced radiator/water pump capacity.
entries.cta: Get quotes for C2L Renault 12 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol (Gordini)
entries.history: Rare Gordini-spec hemispherical head unit with twin Weber 40DCO carburettors; collector-grade performance rebuild only.
commonFailures: Twin Weber carburettor synchronization - complex induction system demands specialist tuning expertise.
High-lift camshaft wear - performance valve train components require precise clearances and premium lubrication.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 807-G Renault 12 engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Renault 12 engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Renault 12 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault 12's Cléon-Fonte engines are robust but age-related failures are common after 40+ years. Head gasket degradation, timing chain tensioner wear, and carburettor ethanol damage dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Renault 12 Head Gasket Failure - Why Cléon-Fonte Engines Leak Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Renault 12 L, TL, TS, Estate (1969-1980, 810/C1H/C1J engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Age-hardened gasket material combined with thermal cycling causes seal failure between cylinder head and block, allowing coolant/oil mixing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket set, pressure test head, reassemble with new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/head undamaged; 2-4 year expectancy on vintage engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply fitted rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket kit, new seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage/vintage units; addresses root cause with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a Renault 12 valued at £2,000-£6,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better long-term value than repeated gasket repairs. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded gasket kits for any Cléon-Fonte engine showing signs of head gasket failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 810/C1H engine prices for your Renault 12

problems.h4: Renault 12 Timing Chain Noise - Cold Start Rattle Fix
problems.affectedModels: Renault 12 TL, TS, TR (1970-1980, 810/C1H engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design loses pressure over time; chain slack causes rattling at cold start and potential timing jump if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing; test run
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix viable if caught early; 3-5 year expectancy with upgraded tensioner

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Timing
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine includes upgraded tensioner assembly, new chain, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines; addresses multiple wear points simultaneously

problems.recommendation: If your Renault 12 shows cold-start chain rattle but runs smoothly when warm, a tensioner upgrade may suffice. However, if the engine has exceeded 80k miles or shows other wear signs, a rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components offers superior long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault 12 timing chain repair or replacement

problems.h4: Renault 12 Carburettor Ethanol Damage - Poor Running Fix
problems.affectedModels: All Renault 12 variants with Weber/Solex carburettors (1969-1980)
problems.rootCause: Modern E10 ethanol fuel degrades vintage rubber seals, diaphragms, and jets in Weber 32DIR/Solex carburettors, causing flooding, lean running, or stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip carb, replace ethanol-resistant seals/jets, recalibrate, test on rolling road
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective for otherwise healthy engines; 2-3 year expectancy with E10-compatible parts

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Carb Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine supplied with ethanol-adapted carburettor or conversion kit option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for comprehensive solution; addresses carb issues plus underlying engine wear

problems.recommendation: For Renault 12 owners using modern E10 fuel, we recommend either a carburettor rebuild with ethanol-resistant components or specifying an ethanol-adapted carburettor with your rebuilt engine. This preventative step protects your investment against recurring fuel-system issues.
problems.cta: Compare Renault 12 carburettor rebuild vs engine replacement options

problems.h4: Renault 12 Low Compression - Worn Rings or Bore Damage
problems.affectedModels: High-mileage Renault 12 TL, TS, Estate (any Cléon-Fonte engine code)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear or cylinder bore scoring reduces compression, causing power loss, oil consumption, and difficult starting.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace rings, hone bores, new gaskets; requires specialist machining
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is sound; 3-5 year expectancy with proper break-in

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Pistons
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new pistons, rings, honed bores, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; addresses all wear points with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: If compression testing confirms low readings across multiple cylinders, a full rebuilt engine typically offers better value and reliability than a partial top-end rebuild on a 40+ year old Cléon-Fonte block.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault 12 low compression engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault 12
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common failure patterns for Renault 12 Cléon-Fonte engines. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault 12 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned, or used replacement engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today and get your classic Renault 12 back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault 12 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault 12 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault 12 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every Cléon-Fonte displacement fitted to the Renault 12 in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Renault 12 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L Cléon-Fonte (1289cc) powered the majority of UK Renault 12 models from launch in 1969 through to end of production in 1980. This robust overhead-valve design with cast-iron block and aluminium head became the backbone of the range, offering economical running and straightforward maintenance for L, TL and early TS variants.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle

items.title: Renault 12 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L Cléon-Fonte (1397cc) was primarily fitted to export-market Renault 12 derivatives including Argentine and Turkish Toros models. While mechanically similar to the 1.3L unit, the larger bore requires verification of intake/exhaust manifold bolt patterns before ordering for UK-fitment applications.
commonFailurePoints: Manifold compatibility differences vs UK spec
cooling system corrosion if incorrect antifreeze used

items.title: Renault 12 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L Cléon-Fonte (1565cc) powered performance-oriented TS and the rare Gordini variants. Higher compression ratios and, on Gordini models, hemispherical cylinder heads with twin Weber carburettors delivered spirited performance but demand specialist rebuilding expertise and upgraded cooling system verification.
commonFailurePoints: High-compression piston wear on performance tunes

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Renault 12 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Renault 12 specialists.
headingLines: Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

engineYears.h2: Renault 12 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major Cléon-Fonte engine changes across the Renault 12 UK production timeline (1969-1980), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Renault 12 launch introduced the robust 1.3L Cléon-Fonte 810 engine powering base L and early TL variants with bench-seat interior configuration.
keyChanges: UK market launch with right-hand drive L and TL variants
Under-dash handbrake and bench seat configuration on early models
Single-choke Weber 32DIR carburettor fitted to all variants
knownFor: Simple, robust engineering ideal for classic restoration projects
Early models command collector premium for originality
Engine mounts standardised across range aiding replacement fitment

years.description: Introduction of higher-compression TS variant with revised carburettor jetting and the TR automatic transmission option expanded the Renault 12 range.
keyChanges: TS performance variant launched with higher-compression 1.3L tune
TR variant introduced with Renault electronic 3-speed automatic transmission
Revised carburettor jetting for improved emissions compliance
knownFor: TS variant offers spirited performance while retaining Cléon-Fonte reliability
Automatic TR models require flexplate/flywheel compatibility verification
Mid-production engines benefit from incremental manufacturing refinements
years.cta: Get quotes for 1973-1975 Renault 12 replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault 12 TS engine replacement • Renault 12 automatic engine compatibility • Renault 12 1974 engine code

years.description: 1975 facelift brought revised dashboard and lighting while late-production engines adopted C1H code with updated cylinder head casting for improved reliability.
keyChanges: 1975 facelift: revised dashboard, rectangular lamps, updated grille
Late-production engines may carry C1H code denoting revised head casting
Final UK production year 1980; parts availability optimal for this period
mainEngines: C1H - 1.3L petrol (TL / TS / TR / Estate late production)
C2L - 1.6L petrol (TS performance / select high-spec applications)
knownFor: C1H engines functionally identical to 810 but with revised head casting for improved durability
Facelift models benefit from latest production refinements and optimal parts availability
Gordini variants highly collectible; specialist rebuilds command premium pricing

faq.h2: Renault 12 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault 12 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt Renault 12 engine prices range from £950-£1,450 for standard 1.3L Cléon-Fonte units, rising to £1,600-£2,400 for performance TS/Gordini 1.6L variants. Used engines start around £400-£650 but carry shorter warranties.
keyPoints: Standard 1.3L rebuilt: £950-£1,450 supply only
Performance 1.6L rebuilt: £1,600-£2,400 supply only
Used engines: £400-£650 with 3-month warranty
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£650 depending on location
items.cta: Get your Renault 12 engine replacement quote today

items.question: What engine codes fit my Renault 12?
items.answer: UK Renault 12 models use Cléon-Fonte petrol engines: 810/C1H for 1.3L L/TL/TS variants, C2L/807-G for 1.6L performance applications. Export models may use C1J/C2J/C3J 1.4L units requiring manifold verification.
keyPoints: 810/C1H: 1.3L base engines for L/TL/TS (1969-1980)
C2L/807-G: 1.6L performance for TS/Gordini
C1J/C2J: 1.4L export units, verify fitment before ordering
Always confirm engine code via VIN or chassis plate
items.cta: Verify your Renault 12 engine code with our free checker

items.question: Can I fit a 1.4L or 1.6L engine in my 1.3L Renault 12?
items.answer: Physically possible but requires mounting bracket modifications, exhaust system adaptation, and carburettor recalibration. We recommend consulting a Renault 12 specialist before attempting displacement upgrades.
keyPoints: Mounting points differ between 1.3L and larger Cléon-Fonte units
Exhaust manifold bolt patterns may not align without adaptation
Carburettor jetting and ignition timing require recalibration
Specialist conversion quotes available through our network
items.cta: Get a bespoke Renault 12 engine swap quote from specialists

items.question: How long does a Renault 12 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Engine dispatch typically takes 3-5 days for common 810/C1H codes or 7-14 days for rare Gordini units. Fitting by a specialist garage requires 1-2 days depending on ancillary work required.
keyPoints: Common codes (810/C1H): 3-5 day dispatch from stock
Rare codes (807-G/Gordini): 7-14 day specialist rebuild lead time
Fitting labour: 1-2 days at approved Renault 12 specialist
Allow extra time for shipping to remote UK locations
items.cta: Find a Renault 12 specialist fitter near you with live availability

items.question: Do you supply Renault 12 engines with fitting?
items.answer: Yes, we partner with UK-wide Renault classic specialists for supply & fit service. Enter your postcode to find approved fitting centres with live availability and transparent pricing for your variant.
keyPoints: Supply & fit pricing includes engine, labour, fluids and road test
Vetted specialists with proven Renault 12 replacement experience
Single-point warranty covering both engine and installation work
Postcode lookup finds nearest approved fitter with pricing
items.cta: Enter postcode to find Renault 12 supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault 12 replacement engine?
items.answer: All reconditioned and rebuilt Renault 12 engines carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines include a 3-month warranty. Full terms provided pre-purchase with your chosen supplier.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/recon engines: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Used engines: 3-month warranty as standard
Warranty valid only when correct engine code supplied
Documentation provided for MoT and insurance purposes
items.cta: Compare Renault 12 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I know which Renault 12 engine I need?
items.answer: Check your vehicle registration document, locate the engine bay stamping on the block rear face, or use our free compatibility checker with your VIN or chassis number for instant verification.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block rear face (driver's side)
Registration document lists variant and engine size
VIN/chassis number lookup via our free compatibility tool
Photo verification service available for uncertain cases
items.cta: Use our Renault 12 engine code checker for instant verification

items.question: Are Renault 12 engines compatible with Dacia 1300 or Toros models?
items.answer: Dacia 1300 engines (Romanian production) use identical 810/C1H codes and are fully compatible. Turkish Toros models require intake/exhaust manifold bolt pattern verification before ordering.
keyPoints: Dacia 1300: identical Cléon-Fonte codes, direct fitment
Turkish Toros: verify manifold bolt patterns vs UK spec
Argentine C1J/C2J units may require adaptation components
Our specialists confirm cross-market compatibility pre-dispatch
items.cta: Verify export model Renault 12 engine compatibility with our team

items.question: Can I return a Renault 12 engine if it doesn't fit?
items.answer: Yes, 14-day return window applies if engine does not fit due to our compatibility error. Customer ordering errors may incur restocking fees; always verify engine code before dispatch.
keyPoints: 14-day return policy for compatibility errors by our team
Customer verification errors may incur restocking fees
Always confirm engine code via VIN/chassis before ordering
Photo verification service reduces fitment risk

items.question: Do you offer finance for Renault 12 engine replacements?
items.answer: 0% finance available on rebuilt engines over £1,500. Apply in 60 seconds during checkout for instant decision; spread the cost of your Renault 12 engine replacement without interest.

60-second online application with instant decision
Flexible repayment terms to suit classic car budgets
No impact on quote comparison or supplier selection
